samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_16bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Chip clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="start">Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="ain" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand (a).</input>
        <input id="bin" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier (b).</input>
        <output id="yout" width_description="[31:0]">Product output signal.</output>
        <output id="done">Chip output flag signal. Defined as 1 indicates the completion of the multiplication operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">16</parameter>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">16</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <task>
            <description>Shift and accumulate operation.</description>
            <implementation>
                <step>On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the shift count register (i) is updated.</step>
                <step>On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is updated.</step>
                <step>On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs the shift and accumulate operation.</step>
            </implementation>
        </task>
        <step>Reset the module by setting rst_n to 0. The multiplicand register (areg), multiplier register (breg), and product register (yout_r) are reset to 0.</step>
        <step>When the start signal is set to 1, the multiplication operation begins.</step>
        <step>When the shift count register (i) is 0, the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) are stored in the respective registers (areg and breg).</step>
        <step>For shift counts greater than 0 and less than 17, if the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high, the product register (yout_r) accumulates the shifted value of the multiplier register (breg) by shifting it left by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions.</step>
        <step>Reset the shift count register (i) to 0 when the start signal is inactive.</step>
        <step>Set the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 1 when the shift count register (i) is equal to 16.</step>
        <step>Reset the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0 when the shift count register (i) is equal to 17.</step>
        <step>Assign the product output (yout) the value of the product register (yout_r).</step>
    </implementation>
</module>
